mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation rise in the housing market index (HOUST) over the next 5 trading days signals an increase in housing demand that boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if HOUST does not exceed its 2-standard-deviation threshold within the next 5 trading days, or if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 12-month moving average signals a supply-side shock that reduces capacity utilization and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if INDPRO does not decline below its 12-month moving average within the next 3 months, or if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-standard-deviation rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) over the next 60 trading days signals a labor market downturn that reduces consumer spending and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if UNRATE does not rise by at least 1 standard deviation within the next 60 trading days, or if the 60-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation rise in the housing market index (HOUST) and a 1-standard-deviation rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) over the next 20 trading days signal an increase in housing demand and a labor market downturn that boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if HOUST does not exceed its 2-standard-deviation threshold and UNRATE does not rise by at least 1 standard deviation within the next 20 trading days, or if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) below 2% signals a monetary easing cycle that reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if FEDFUNDS does not decline below 2% within the next 3 months, or if the 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-standard-deviation rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above its 12-month moving average signals a supply-side shock that increases capacity utilization and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if INDPRO does not rise by at least 1 standard deviation above its 12-month moving average within the next 60 trading days, or if the 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation rise in the housing market index (HOUST) and a 1-standard-deviation rise in the real personal spending and income (RSAFS) over the next 20 trading days signal an increase in housing demand and consumer spending that boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if HOUST does not exceed its 2-standard-deviation threshold and RSAFS does not rise by at least 1 standard deviation within the next 20 trading days, or if the 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-standard-deviation rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) and a 1-standard-deviation rise in the initial unemployment claims (PAYEMS) over the next 60 trading days signal a labor market downturn that reduces consumer spending and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if UNRATE does not rise by at least 1 standard deviation and PAYEMS does not rise by at least 1 standard deviation within the next 60 trading days, or if the 60-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
